item.title: A message from Mme Perron:
item.description: There will be a brief Europe Trip meeting today at 10:35 in room 125. All travelers MUST attend. Arrangements need to be made for pick up Charcuterie Boards this afternoon and Chocolate Almond Sale plans will begin. Please be on time. Any staff member who purchased a charcuterie board will find them available to take home after work in the fridge in the MAIN FLOOR staffroom please do not forget to take them home as they need to be refrigerated.

item.title: A message from Mr. Vonesch:
item.description: Grade 10,11,12 classes, please note that after announcements you should have a small piece of paper on which you will write a prayer intention: write the names of people you want to pray for, or a situation you want to pray for. Please do this immediately after announcements. Grade 7,8,9 classes will do this as you arrive to 2nd period. Please bring this paper with you to the prayer service this morning, and you will be instructed at that time.

item.title: A message from Mr. Benoit:
item.description: Congratulations to our 7&8 hockey teams on an outstanding tournament yesterday in Russell! Both teams finished the day undefeated. They played hard, demonstarted great sportsmanship, and represented our school with pride. Great job everyone!

item.title: A message from Mme. Bourdon:
item.description: A ski pass for Les Sommets was found on the ground yesterday. If this is yours, please see Mme Bourdon to claim the card.

item.title: A message from Guidance:
item.description: Are you interested in Business or Entrepreneurship? Grade 11 students have the opportunity to visit Algonquin College later this month to take part in hands-on workshops that provide a great experience about what college programs would be like. Send Mr Spence a Teams message if you're interested.
